prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `c64f1187`.
codex_response:
Implemented `c64f1187` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_c64f1187/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_c64f1187/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_c64f1187/generator.py). The rule is: read the top legend where each color labels a blue 2x2 motif, crop the lower gray 2x2-tile lattice, and replace each tagged tile with that legend motif recolored to the tile’s tag color; untagged tiles become black. The Opus summary hint matched the official examples, so I used it and did not add `notes.md`. No new shared primitive was needed.

Validation passed end-to-end: official verifier `3/3`, shared build produced `1000` examples, generated verification passed `1000/1000`, and the previews look on-family. The generator explicitly builds 3-4 distinct legend entries, a 3-4 by 4-5 gray lattice with bottom-right color tags and empty tiles, then derives the cropped output from that latent structure.
